en.title: When Does Your Small Business Need General Liability?
en.excerpt: From day one. If your business interacts with customers or the public, GL coverage is essential — and more affordable than you think.
en.body:
The answer is: from the day you open your doors.

General Liability (GL) insurance protects your business from third-party claims of bodily injury, property damage, and personal injury. If a customer slips at your location, if you accidentally damage a client's property, or if someone sues you over an advertising claim — GL is what pays.

## Who Requires It?

Most commercial landlords require GL before signing a lease. General contractors require it from every subcontractor before work begins. Many corporate clients require proof of coverage before signing a contract.

## What Does It Cover?

Bodily injury to a third party. Property damage you cause to others. Legal defense costs. Medical payments. Personal and advertising injury.

## What Does It Not Cover?

Your own employees (that is Workers' Comp), your own property (that is commercial property insurance), or professional errors (that is Professional Liability/E&O).

## How Much Does It Cost?

For most small businesses in Florida, a GL policy runs between $400 and $1,200 per year depending on industry, revenue, and claims history.

It is not an expense. It is what keeps one incident from becoming a business-ending lawsuit.
